info.title: ready2order Public API
info.description:
# First Steps

## Important
You **cannot** use the ready2order API until you complete the Authorization steps and get an **Account Token**.

Every request after setup **must** include this header:

```http
Authorization: Bearer <ACCOUNT_TOKEN>
```

---

## Authentication 

ready2order uses **token-based authentication**.

- Every API request **must** include an `Authorization` header
- The header **must** be spelled exactly as:

```http
Authorization
```

(Yes, capitalization matters.)

There are **three different tokens**.  
You must obtain and use them **in this exact order**.

---

## Token Types 

### 1. Developer Token

**What it is**  
Identifies **you** as a developer. This token is unique to you and must be kept confidential. 

**How to get it**
1. Go to https://api.ready2order.com
2. Sign up as a developer
3. Check your email â your **Developer Token** is included

**When you use it**  
Only to request a **Grant Access Token**.

---

### 2ï¸ Grant Access Token

**What it is**  
A **temporary** token used to request access to a ready2order account.

**Important**
- Valid for **10 minutes**
- If it expires â request a new one

---

### 3 Account Token 

**What it is**  
Grants access to **real account data**, such as:
- products
- product groups
- customers
- invoices
- orders
- coupons

**Every account API request requires this token.**

```http
Authorization: Bearer <ACCOUNT_TOKEN>
```

---

## Getting Started 

### Step 1 â Obtain a Developer Token

- Register at https://api.ready2order.com
- No authentication required yet
- Save your **Developer Token**

---

### Step 2 â Request a Grant Access Token

**Endpoint**
```http
POST /v1/developerToken/grantAccessToken
```

**Headers**
```http
Authorization: Bearer <DEVELOPER_TOKEN>
```

**Request Body (optional)**
```json
{
  "callbackUri": "https://your-app.com/callback"
}
```

**Response**
You will receive:
- `grantAccessToken`
- `grantAccessUri`

ð **Copy the `grantAccessUri`** â you need it immediately.

---

### Step 3 â Obtain an Account Token

1. Open the `grantAccessUri` in a browser
2. Log in with the **ready2order account** you want access to
3. Click **Approve**

If you provided a `callbackUri`, you will be redirected with these parameters:

- `accountToken` â **THIS IS THE TOKEN YOU WANT**
- `grantAccessToken`
- `status` (`approved` or `denied`)

**Example redirect**
```text
https://your-app.com/callback?
accountToken=<ACCOUNT_TOKEN>
&grantAccessToken=<GRANT_ACCESS_TOKEN>
&status=approved
```

â ï¸ If you do not approve within **10 minutes**, repeat **Step 2**.

---

## Using the API 

Every request that accesses account data **must** include:

```http
Authorization: Bearer <ACCOUNT_TOKEN>
```

---

## Rate Limits

- **60 requests per minute**
- Per **Account Token**
- Exceeded limit â HTTP `429`

---

## Webhooks

### What Are Webhooks?

Webhooks notify your application **automatically** when something happens in ready2order.

No polling required.

---

### Available Events

| Event | Description |
|------|------------|
| `product.created` | Product created |
| `product.updated` | Product updated |
| `product.deleted` | Product deleted |
| `productGroup.created` | Product group created |
| `productGroup.updated` | Product group updated |
| `productGroup.deleted` | Product group deleted |
| `invoice.created` | Invoice / receipt created |
| `orderItem.created` | Order item created |
| `coupon.created` | Coupon created |
| `coupon.updated` | Coupon updated |
| `couponTransaction.created` | Coupon used |

---

### Webhook Payloads

- **Created / Updated events**  
  â Full object included (no extra API call needed)

- **Deleted events**  
  â Only resource IDs included

---

### Setting Up Webhooks

1. Configure your webhook URL via  
   `/webhook`

2. Subscribe to webhook events via  
   `/webhook/events`

---

## Wrap up

1. **Developer Token** â identifies you
2. **Grant Access Token** â asks permission
3. **Account Token** â lets you do real work

If something doesnât work, itâs usually because:
- the wrong token is used
- the `Authorization` header is missing
- the Grant Access Token expired

post.description: This endpoint can be used to revoke the currently used API Account Token.

get.summary: Find Accounting Financial Year by Id

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Use one of the possible export formats: bmd, bmd-ntcs, datev, sap, rzl

parameters.name: year
parameters.description: Year for which the export should be generated (optional)

parameters.name: month
parameters.description: Month for which the export should be generated (optional)

parameters.name: day
parameters.description: Day for which the export should be generated (optional)

parameters.name: exportCashbook
parameters.description: Whether to include cashbook transactions in the export

parameters.name: dataSource
parameters.description: Type of data-aggregation to use. Possible value are: invoice, eachItem, dailyReport_productGroups, dailyReport_accountingCode

parameters.name: financialYear
parameters.description:
If format is datev the number of the financialYear has to be provided. See GET /accounting/financialYears

_* Required when `format` is `datev`._

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
If format is datev the start date of the financialYear has to be provided. See GET /accounting/financialYears

_* Required when `financialYear` is provided._

parameters.name: to
parameters.description:
If format is datev the end date of the financialYear has to be provided. See GET /accounting/financialYears

_* Required when `financialYear` is provided._

masterCustomer.description: Customer into which the list of customerIds should be merged into.

customerIds.description: List of customers which shall be merged into masterCustomer.

authorizationCallbackUri.description: The callback to which the merchant will be redirected if access will be granted.

discountGroup_description.description: The description of the discount group

discountGroup_active.description: Whether the discount group is active or not

discountGroup_name.description: The name of the discount group

discountGroup_description.description: The description of the discount group

discountGroup_active.description: Whether the discount group is active or not

discountGroup_name.description: The name of the discount group

discountGroup_id.description: The id of the discount group

discount_active.description: Whether the discount is active or not

discountGroup_id.description: The id of the discount group

discount_active.description: Whether the discount is active or not

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query; the value to search by.

parameters.name: dateField
parameters.description:
Date field the query should filter by.

__Values__
`daily_report` Filter by the start date of the related Daily Report
`bill` Filter by the billâs creation date

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description:
Marks the start date for filtering. Can be a whole date (account's timestamp is used by default) or a datetime (ISO 8601).

`2024-10-23` or `2023-12-31T13:00:00Z`

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description:
Marks the end date for filtering. Can be a whole date (account's timestamp is used by default) or a datetime (ISO 8601).

`2024-11-21` or `2023-12-31T13:00:00Z`

paymentMethod_id.description: PaymentMethod ID. Whether the bill is marked as paid depends on the payment method type â payment methods with markAsPaid=true will automatically mark the bill as paid.

printer_id.description: Printer ID on which the bill should be printed

printingProfile_id.description: PrintingProfile ID on which the bill should be printed.

invoice_printCount.description: Number of copies to be printed (where 1 is the original receipt)

invoice_textBeforeItemsTable.description: Text shown before items table on bill PDF

invoice_text.description: Text shown after items table on bill PDF

invoice_externalReferenceNumber.description: External reference number for the bill

invoice_totalTip.description: Total tip amount for the invoice.

createPDF.description: Whether to create and return PDF or not

invoice_showRecipient.description: Show additional lines on invoice receipt

items.description:
List of items

_* Required if `orderItem_ids` is not provided._

orderItem_ids.description:
List of orderItemIds to process the invoice for

_* Required if `items` is not provided._

order_enablePrinting.description: Whether to print the order receipts (foods, drinks) for the bill (if configured)

storno_reason.description: Some reason why bill was deleted

invoice_externalReferenceNumber.description: Some external reference number for that bill

post.description: Add bill to printer queue for specific printer

printer_id.description:
Printer ID where to print the bill

_* Required if none of `printingProfile_id` are provided._

printingProfile_id.description:
PrintingProfile ID on which the bill should be printed.

_* Required if none of `printer_id` are provided._

msg.example: Bill was added to printer queue

get.description: Returns signed S3 URL. Optionally regenerates pdf if `regeneratePDF` is set to `true`

parameters.name: groupBy
parameters.description: allowed values: table_id or product_id or both

parameters.name: trainingsMode
parameters.description: retrieve results for trainingsMode or liveMde

table_id.description: Table on which the orders are booked

training_mode.description: Whether to place the order in training- or normal-mode

item_orderGroup.description: Order group on the table to book on. Use this if multiple parties are sharing the same table.

item_discountable.description: Whether the product should be applicable to apply discounts on it

item_mergeable.description: Whether the product should be merged into one line on the final invoice.

post.description:
Transfers unbilled order items from one table to another.
Pass specific `order_ids` to transfer a subset, or omit the field to transfer all unbilled items on the source table.

source_table_id.description: ID of the table to transfer order items from

target_table_id.description: ID of the table to transfer order items to

target_order_group_id.description: Order group on the target table to assign transferred items to

target_order_group_name.description: Name of the target order group (optional)

order_ids.description: IDs of the specific order items to transfer. Omit or pass an empty array to transfer every unbilled item on the source table.

training_mode.description: Whether to operate in training mode

payment_description.description: The description of the payment method

paymentType_id.description: The payment type id of the payment method

payment_name.description: The name of the payment method

payment_description.description: The description of the payment method

paymentType_id.description: The payment type id of the payment method

payment_name.description: The name of the payment method

get.summary: Find Payment Method Type by Id

product_vat.description: VAT-Rate in decimals. Provide either product_vat or product_vat_id

product_vat_id.description: ID of VAT-rate (see /vat-rates). Provide either product_vat or product_vat_id

product_priceIncludesVat.description: Whether price is provided included or excluding VAT

product_type.description: In case of adding new sidedishes or extras you need to provide the product_type. For example, if you're adding 'Fries' as a side you need to provide 'food'.

parameters.name: productgroup_id
parameters.description: get all products for this productgroup_id

parameters.name: itemNumber
parameters.description: search for a product with the given product_itemNumber

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: search for a product with the given product_name

parameters.name: barcode
parameters.description: search for a product with the given product_barcode

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: search for a product by keywords

parameters.name: updatedAfter
parameters.description: Only return list of products which have been updated after a given timestamp

product_vat.description:
VAT-Rate in decimals. Provide either product_vat or product_vat_id

_* Required if `product_vat_id` is not provided._

product_vat_id.description:
ID of VAT-rate (see /vat-rates). Provide either product_vat or product_vat_id

_* Required if `product_vat` is not provided._

product_priceIncludesVat.description: Whether price is provided included or excluding VAT

product_type.description: In case of adding new sidedishes or extras you need to provide the product_type. For example, if you're adding 'Fries' as a side you need to provide 'food'.

product_id.description: You can create a product variation for a certain product by setting the product_base-object with the product_id of the base-product. To UPDATE or DELETE a product variation use the /products endpoint as a product variation is like normal product.

product_vat.description: VAT-Rate in decimals. Provide either product_vat or product_vat_id

product_vat_id.description: ID of VAT-rate (see /vat-rates). Provide either product_vat or product_vat_id

product_priceIncludesVat.description: Whether price is provided included or excluding VAT

product_type.description: In case of adding new sidedishes or extras you need to provide the product_type. For example, if you're adding 'Fries' as a side you need to provide 'food'.

product_id.description: You can create a product variation for a certain product by setting the product_base-object with the product_id of the base-product. To UPDATE or DELETE a product variation use the /products endpoint as a product variation is like normal product.

get.summary: Get All Customer Category Product Prices

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the terminal transaction

parameters.name: txId
parameters.description: ID of the terminal transaction to abort

get.description: This endpoint returns a list of all available VatRates for this account
